title: tinfoil-conformance capabilities
description: Self-description emitted by `tinfoil-conformance capabilities`. Read once per SDK by the harness, used to skip fixtures that require unsupported knobs.

schema_version.description: Conformance contract version this binary speaks.

sdk_version.description: SDK release version embedded in this binary.

trust_root_loading.description: Whether the SDK accepts an inline trust root, or only uses its embedded copy.

verification_time_override.description: Where the verifier sources time-of-verification from: 'supported' (uses policy.verification_time_unix); 'system-clock-only' (Date.now() — fixtures rot); 'bundle-supplied-only' (cert NotBefore / Rekor integratedTime from the bundle, hermetic on system clock but caller's verification_time_unix is ignored).

predicate_types_understood.description: Tinfoil predicate type URIs this SDK can extract a Measurement from.

legacy_bundle_format_supported.description: True if the SDK accepts pre-v0.3 bundles where the cert lives at verificationMaterial.x509CertificateChain.certificates[0].rawBytes instead of .certificate.rawBytes. Optional and forward-compat — real production is v0.3-only.

accepts_multi_tlog_entries.description: True if the SDK accepts bundles with >1 Rekor tlog entries (SPEC §5.2 #3 says 'at least 1'). False indicates the SDK is over-strict — it hardcodes exactly-1 — which is a known sigstore-python (and historically sigstore-go) limitation.

oidc_issuer_v2_preferred.description: True if the SDK prefers the OIDC Issuer V2 extension (OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.57264.1.8) over V1 (.1.1) when both are present in the leaf cert. SPEC §5.3 doesn't pin precedence; Rust and JS prefer V2, sigstore-python prefers V1.

scts_count_distinguish_missing_vs_duplic.description: True if the SDK distinguishes SCT_INSUFFICIENT (zero SCTs) from SCT_DUPLICATE_LOG (two SCTs from same log) at the rejection-code level. sigstore-python collapses both into the same 'expected one certificate timestamp' error and can't tell them apart.

rejects_duplicate_sct_log.description: True if the SDK rejects bundles where the leaf cert carries two SCTs from the same CT log id. sigstore-go silently accepts duplicate SCTs.

checks_only_subject_0.description: True if the SDK only checks subject[0].digest.sha256 (SPEC §5.4 wording 'If the subject array contains multiple entries, only the first entry is checked'). sigstore-go iterates ALL subjects when matching the artifact digest, accepting bundles where subject[0] is bad but subject[1] matches.

in_toto_statement_tolerates_extra_fields.description: True if the SDK tolerates unknown top-level fields on the in-toto statement (forward-compat). sigstore-go's strict in-toto parser rejects unknown fields.

compare_multiplatform_to_tdx_supported.description: True if the SDK implements the SPEC §7.3.2 SnpTdxMultiPlatformV1 → TdxGuestV2 comparison (and its §7.3.4 reverse). tinfoil-js's compareMeasurements currently handles only same-type and MP↔SEV; MP↔TDX comparisons throw 'incompatible measurement types' — false until the lib gains the TDX path.

attestation_sev.description: Capabilities for the verify-attestation-sev stage (SPEC §3 AMD SEV-SNP).

supported.description: True if the SDK can verify SEV-SNP attestation reports given an attestation document + VCEK certificate inline. False = the verify-attestation-sev fixtures skip cleanly.

injected_collateral_supported.description: True if the SDK's SEV verifier accepts an inline VCEK cert instead of fetching from AMD's KDS. All four SDKs do support this — VCEK injection is the canonical API.

extended_checks_supported.description: True if the SDK applies SPEC §3.7 policy validation against policy.expected_*_hex pins (measurement, host_data, report_data, id_key_digest, etc.) and policy.enforce_spec_defaults (guest_policy.debug, reserved bit checks). False = structural verification only.

amd_root_ca_injection_supported.description: True if the SDK's SEV verifier accepts inline ARK + ASK PEMs (via input.amd_root_ca_pem / input.ask_pem) instead of using its embedded AMD chain. Required for Phase 4B-SEV synthetic-violation fixtures that re-sign reports under a synthetic ARK→ASK→VCEK chain. False = the SDK is locked to its embedded ARK_CERT/ASK_CERT (e.g. tinfoil-js, which freezes them as bundled constants); 4B fixtures skip cleanly.

verification_time_override.description: Where the SDK sources VCEK validity time from.

attestation_tdx.description: Capabilities for the verify-attestation-tdx stage (Intel TDX DCAP §4.1, Tinfoil SPEC §4).

supported.description: True if the SDK can verify Intel TDX DCAP quotes when the conformance binary injects the collateral (TCB Info, QE Identity, CRLs). Required for any fixture in the attestation-tdx stage to run; SDKs that lack TDX support (e.g., tinfoil-js whose PredicateType enum doesn't include TdxGuestV2) declare false and the fixtures skip cleanly.

injected_collateral_supported.description: True if the SDK's TDX verifier accepts inline collateral (TCB Info JSON, QE Identity JSON, CRLs) instead of fetching from the network. SDKs that hardcode an Intel PCS Getter without an injection point declare false; their fixtures skip until the lib gains a collateral-injection entry point.

verification_time_override.description: Where the verifier sources time-of-verification from. 'supported' — uses input.expiration_check_date_unix for cert validity, CRL nextUpdate, JSON nextUpdate. 'system-clock-only' — calls datetime.now() unconditionally; fixtures pinning a verification time outside the real-now range skip cleanly. Mirrors the sigstore capability flag.

tcb_evaluation_supported.description: True if the SDK actually runs Intel §4.7 collateral evaluation end-to-end (PCK CRL revocation check, Root CRL check, TCB Info signature + chain + nextUpdate, QE Identity signature + chain + nextUpdate, TCB level comparison). False = the SDK only does structural cryptographic verification (steps 1-5) and rejects policy.tcb_evaluation_required=true with QV_RESULT_TERMINAL_UNSPECIFIED. Phase 2B fixtures targeting collateral-layer rejections gate on this.

public_api_hooks_supported.description: True if execution_mode=public_api fixtures call the SDK's normal public TDX verifier/orchestration path while injecting only external dependencies such as PCS HTTP responses, trust root, and verification time. False means the conformance binary still uses adapter/lower-level methods for TDX.

extended_td_checks_supported.description: True if the SDK applies Intel §2.3.2 / Tinfoil SPEC §4.8 extended TD checks against policy.expected_*_hex pins (TD_ATTRIBUTES, XFAM, MR_SEAM allowlist, MR_SIGNER_SEAM, SEAM_ATTRIBUTES, MRTD, MR_CONFIG_ID, MR_OWNER, MR_OWNER_CONFIG, RTMR3, REPORT_DATA, min TEE_TCB_SVN, QE_VENDOR_ID). Phase 4 fixtures gate on this. SDKs that only extract these fields without enforcing policy declare false.

accepts_non_terminal_tcb_statuses.description: Per Tinfoil SPEC §4.7.7 / Intel §B.1, SDKs MAY accept SWHardeningNeeded, ConfigurationNeeded, and ConfigurationAndSWHardeningNeeded as non-terminal (default-acceptable) statuses. True = SDK accepts these by default (SPEC-aligned permissive default). False = SDK rejects every non-UpToDate status (strict-only policy, e.g. go-tdx-guest's ErrTcbStatus). Phase 3 accept-path fixtures for these statuses gate on this.

enforces_tcb_evaluation_data_number_mini.description: True if the conformance binary enforces SPEC §4.7.11's minimum tcbEvaluationDataNumber (default 18) against TCB Info / QE Identity collateral. False = the binary passes 0 (or doesn't apply the check at all) so older collateral isn't rejected. tinfoil-go's NewTDXGetter enforces this at fetch time but go-tdx-guest's verify path doesn't expose it; tinfoil-python's lib accepts a parameter but the conformance binary passes 0 to compat with Phase 2B+3 fixtures. Fixture 346 gates on this.

policy_fields_supported.description: Per-field map of which policy.* knobs the SDK consults when verifying. False fields cause fixtures that pin those policy values to skip cleanly.

expected_fmspc_hex.description: True if the SDK reads policy.expected_fmspc_hex and rejects a quote whose PCK leaf FMSPC differs. False = the SDK doesn't pin FMSPC; fixture 325-pck-fmspc-mismatch skips.

accepted_qv_results.description: True if the SDK reads policy.accepted_qv_results after deriving the Intel QV result and rejects non-terminal results outside that allowlist with QV_RESULT_NOT_ACCEPTED_BY_POLICY. False = the SDK either has no policy hook or rejects before exposing non-terminal QV results.

platforms_supported.description: Hardware attestation platforms with native verification support.

ehbp.description: Capabilities for the EHBP (Encrypted HTTP Body Protocol) transport stages. EHBP itself is specified at github.com/tinfoilsh/encrypted-http-body-protocol; the Tinfoil binding requirements are in Tinfoil SPEC §14.2.

key_binding_supported.description: True if the SDK enforces Tinfoil SPEC §14.2 EHBP key binding: the HPKE public key used for the encrypted transport MUST equal the attested key carried in report_data[32:64] (report_data layout: [0:32]=TLS SPKI fingerprint, [32:64]=HPKE X25519 public key). A server-offered key that differs from the attested key MUST be rejected (fail closed). The EHBP protocol spec alone trusts the /.well-known/hpke-keys endpoint and does NOT require this binding — it is a Tinfoil-layer requirement. SDKs that do not implement an EHBP transport declare false and the verify-ehbp-key-binding fixtures skip cleanly.

flow_modes_supported.description: End-to-end flow modes (SPEC §11). Relevant for verify-full.

known_quirks.description: Free-form bag of declared deviations from SPEC the SDK is aware of. Used for documentation, not skip logic.
